{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.3": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent correctly identifies three internal contradictions in the text, including a writing error that inverts the claims being made.",
    "R1.3": "The agent accurately flags inconsistencies between the evidence and claims presented in the text.",
    "RX.1": "The agent provides clear explanations for each of the critical issues it identifies, including uncertainty about the deployed policy.",
    "RX.3": "While the output is mostly within scope, there are some minor drifts in the discussion of latency and quality metrics.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces no new errors or harmful content, but there are some minor regressions in the interpretation of the results.",
    "RX.5": "The agent provides some information about the provenance and traceability of the data used to train the classifier, but it is not comprehensive."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.95,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.85,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent correctly identifies three internal contradictions in the text, including a writing error that inverts the claims being made.
rationale.R1.3: The agent accurately flags inconsistencies between the evidence and claims presented in the text.
rationale.RX.1: The agent provides clear explanations for each of the critical issues it identifies, including uncertainty about the deployed policy.
rationale.RX.3: While the output is mostly within scope, there are some minor drifts in the discussion of latency and quality metrics.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces no new errors or harmful content, but there are some minor regressions in the interpretation of the results.
rationale.RX.5: The agent provides some information about the provenance and traceability of the data used to train the classifier, but it is not comprehensive.
